Items.Title: Authentication and Access Control The system must ensure that only authorized users can access protected business functionalities.
Items.Content: BR-01: Authentication and Access Control The system must ensure that only authorized users can access protected business functionalities. Requirements: • The system must require authentication before granting access to business operations • The system must validate user identity for each request • The system must restrict unauthorized access to all protected endpoints Expected Outcome: All business operations are performed only by authenticated and authorized users.

Items.Title: User Authentication
Items.Content:
FR-01: User Authentication Description The system must authenticate users before allowing access to protected resources. 

Inputs • Username / Email • Password Processing • Validate credentials against stored records • Generate authentication token Outputs • Success: Authentication token returned • Failure: Unauthorized response Validation Rules • Credentials must be valid • Invalid login attempts must be rejected

Items.Title: Customer Management The system must provide the ability to manage customer information used in operational and financial processes.
Items.Content:
BR-02: Customer Management The system must provide the ability to manage customer information used in operational and financial processes. Requirements: • The system must allow creation of new customer records • The system must allow updating of existing customer details • The system must allow retrieval of customer information • The system must maintain uniqueness and integrity of customer data 

Expected Outcome: Accurate and consistent customer records are available for all downstream processes.

Items.Title: Customer Record Completeness A customer record must contain all mandatory attributes required to identify and manage the customer in operational and financial processes.
Items.Content: BRULE-08: Customer Record Completeness A customer record must contain all mandatory attributes required to identify and manage the customer in operational and financial processes. Validation Requirements • Customer name must be provided. • Required business contact fields must not be blank if marked mandatory by the domain model.

Items.Title: Customer Requirement for Job Creation A job card must be linked to a valid customer.
Items.Content:
BRULE-13: Customer Requirement for Job Creation A job card must be linked to a valid customer. 

Validation Requirements • Job creation must fail if the customer reference is missing or invalid.

Items.Title: Vendor Management The system must support the management of vendors who provide operational services.
Items.Content: BR-03: Vendor Management The system must support the management of vendors who provide operational services. Requirements: • The system must allow creation and maintenance of vendor records • The system must associate vendors with service types • The system must allow retrieval of vendor information Expected Outcome: Vendors are properly maintained and available for assignment in operational services.

Items.Title: Vendor Record Completeness A vendor record must include the minimum information required to identify the vendor and associate the vendor with supported services.
Items.Content: BRULE-09: Vendor Record Completeness A vendor record must include the minimum information required to identify the vendor and associate the vendor with supported services. Validation Requirements • Vendor name must be provided. • Vendor must be classifiable for operational usage.

Items.Title: Vendor Consistency for Voucher Where the voucher is vendor-specific, the included payable items must align with the vendor associated with the voucher.
Items.Content: BRULE-42: Vendor Consistency for Voucher Where the voucher is vendor-specific, the included payable items must align with the vendor associated with the voucher.

Items.Title: Event Management The system must allow the creation and management of events that group operational activities.
Items.Content: BR-04: Event Management The system must allow the creation and management of events that group operational activities. Requirements: • The system must allow creation of events • The system must allow updating event details • The system must allow retrieval of event information Expected Outcome: Events can be used to logically group and organize job cards.

Items.Title: Event Date Consistency Where an event includes start and end dates, the end date must not occur before the start date.
Items.Content: BRULE-10: Event Date Consistency Where an event includes start and end dates, the end date must not occur before the start date. Validation Requirements • EndDate >= StartDate

Items.Title: Job Card Management The system must support the creation and lifecycle management of job cards representing operational work.
Items.Content:
BR-05: Job Card Management The system must support the creation and lifecycle management of job cards representing operational work. Requirements: • The system must allow creation of job cards linked to customers 

• The system must allow updating of job card details • The system must allow retrieval of job cards • The system must allow deletion of job cards under valid conditions • The system must support opening and closing of job cards Expected Outcome: Operational work is tracked through structured job cards with controlled lifecycle states.

Items.Title: Cost Management The system must ensure accurate tracking and aggregation of costs associated with service items.
Items.Content:
BR-07: Cost Management The system must ensure accurate tracking and aggregation of costs associated with service items. Requirements: • The system must maintain cost values for each service item • The system must calculate total cost at job level • The system must update cost calculations dynamically based on changes Expected Outcome: 

Financial values accurately reflect operational activities.

Items.Title: Operational Service Management The system must support recording and management of service items associated with job cards.
Items.Content: BR-06: Operational Service Management The system must support recording and management of service items associated with job cards. Requirements: • The system must allow adding service items to a job card • The system must allow updating service items • The system must allow removal of service items • The system must support multiple service types including transportation, hotel, visa, and miscellaneous Expected Outcome: All operational activities are captured as structured service records linked to job cards.

Items.Title: Batch Processing The system must group operational service items into batches for financial processing.
Items.Content: BR-08: Batch Processing The system must group operational service items into batches for financial processing. Requirements: • The system must allow selection of eligible items for batching • The system must allow creation and management of batches • The system must prevent duplication of items across batches Expected Outcome: Operational items are efficiently grouped for downstream financial workflows.

Items.Title: Batch Integrity All items included in a batch must remain valid and traceable to their source records.
Items.Content: BRULE-37: Batch Integrity All items included in a batch must remain valid and traceable to their source records.

Items.Title: Payment Voucher Management The system must support the creation and management of payment vouchers for vendor payments.
Items.Content: BR-09: Payment Voucher Management The system must support the creation and management of payment vouchers for vendor payments. Requirements: • The system must allow creation of payment vouchers linked to batch items • The system must allow retrieval of payment voucher information • The system must ensure correctness of payment amounts Expected Outcome: Vendor payment obligations are recorded and managed accurately.

Items.Title: Invoice Management The system must support billing of customers through invoices.
Items.Content:
BR-10: Invoice Management The system must support billing of customers through invoices. Requirements: • The system must allow creation of invoices • The system must allow retrieval of invoices 

• The system must allow voiding of invoices under valid conditions Expected Outcome: Customer billing is handled in a structured and controlled manner.

Items.Title: Void Invoice
Items.Content: FR-19: Void Invoice Description The system must allow voiding invoices.

Items.Title: Invoice Source Validity An invoice must be created only from valid billable business content.
Items.Content: BRULE-44: Invoice Source Validity An invoice must be created only from valid billable business content.

Items.Title: Invoice Amount Integrity Invoice totals must accurately reflect the chargeable content from the underlying operational and financial records.
Items.Content: BRULE-45: Invoice Amount Integrity Invoice totals must accurately reflect the chargeable content from the underlying operational and financial records.

Items.Title: Proforma Invoice Management The system must support preliminary billing through proforma invoices.
Items.Content: BR-11: Proforma Invoice Management The system must support preliminary billing through proforma invoices. Requirements: • The system must allow creation of proforma invoices • The system must allow conversion of proforma invoices into final invoices Expected Outcome: Preliminary billing can be performed prior to final financial processing.

Items.Title: Proforma as Non-Final Billing A proforma invoice must be treated as a preliminary billing document and not as a finalized financial settlement record.
Items.Content: BRULE-48: Proforma as Non-Final Billing A proforma invoice must be treated as a preliminary billing document and not as a finalized financial settlement record.

Items.Title: Proforma Conversion Control Where conversion from proforma invoice to final invoice is supported, the conversion must preserve business consistency and avoid duplicated billing.
Items.Content: BRULE-49: Proforma Conversion Control Where conversion from proforma invoice to final invoice is supported, the conversion must preserve business consistency and avoid duplicated billing.

Items.Title: Receipt Management The system must support recording of payments received from customers.
Items.Content: BR-12: Receipt Management The system must support recording of payments received from customers. Requirements: • The system must allow creation of receipts • The system must allow linking of receipts to invoices • The system must support voiding of receipts Expected Outcome: Payments are accurately recorded and linked to corresponding financial documents.

Items.Title: Receipt Amount Limit The cumulative total of receipts applied to an invoice must not exceed the allowed receivable amount for that invoice unless overpayment handling is explicitly supported.
Items.Content: BRULE-52: Receipt Amount Limit The cumulative total of receipts applied to an invoice must not exceed the allowed receivable amount for that invoice unless overpayment handling is explicitly supported.

Items.Title: Receipt Traceability A receipt must remain traceable to the invoice or financial document against which it was recorded.
Items.Content: BRULE-54: Receipt Traceability A receipt must remain traceable to the invoice or financial document against which it was recorded.

Items.Title: Receipt Void Preservation Voiding a receipt must preserve transaction history and audit visibility.
Items.Content: BRULE-55: Receipt Void Preservation Voiding a receipt must preserve transaction history and audit visibility.

Items.Title: Reporting and Analytics The system must provide reporting capabilities for operational and financial data.
Items.Content:
BR-13: Reporting and Analytics The system must provide reporting capabilities for operational and financial data. Requirements: • The system must generate operational reports • The system must generate historical reports 

• The system must generate uninvoiced reports • The system must generate profit and loss reports Expected Outcome: Business users have access to accurate and meaningful insights. 2.4 Business Requirement Dependencies • Job Card Management depends on Customer and Event Management • Service Management depends on Job Card Management • Cost Management depends on Service Management • Batch Processing depends on Service Management • Payment Voucher Management depends on Batch Processing • Invoice Management depends on Job Card and Service data • Receipt Management depends on Invoice Management • Reporting depends on all modules 2.5 Business Requirement Constraints • Business operations must follow defined process sequences • Financial transactions must be traceable and auditable • Invalid operations must be rejected by the system • Data consistency must be maintained across all modules 2.6 Business Requirement Acceptance Criteria Each business requirement is considered satisfied when: • The functionality is implemented according to the defined requirement • All relevant business rules are enforced 

• The system prevents invalid operations • Outputs match expected business outcomes • Data consistency is maintained across modules 3. Functional Requirements

Items.Title: Reporting Accuracy All reports must reflect the current valid state of the underlying business data according to the reporting context.
Items.Content: BRULE-56: Reporting Accuracy All reports must reflect the current valid state of the underlying business data according to the reporting context.

Items.Title: Token Validation
Items.Content:
FR-02: Token Validation Description The system must validate authentication tokens for all protected API requests. Inputs • JWT token Processing • Verify token signature • Check token expiration • Extract user identity Outputs • Success: Request proceeds 

• Failure: Unauthorized response Validation Rules • Token must not be expired • Token must be valid and untampered

Items.Title: Token Validity The system must reject expired, malformed, tampered, or otherwise invalid authentication tokens.
Items.Content: BRULE-06: Token Validity The system must reject expired, malformed, tampered, or otherwise invalid authentication tokens. Validation Requirement • Token structure, expiration, and signature must be validated before request processing.

Items.Title: Create Customer
Items.Content: FR-03: Create Customer Description The system must allow creation of a new customer record. Inputs • Customer Name • Contact Details Processing • Validate input data • Store customer record Outputs • Success: Customer created with ID • Failure: Validation error Validation Rules • Name is mandatory • Duplicate entries must be prevented (if applicable)

Items.Title: Create Vendor
Items.Content:
FR-06: Create Vendor Description The system must allow creation of vendor records. 

Inputs • Vendor Name • Service Type Processing • Validate and store vendor Outputs • Success: Vendor created

Items.Title: Create Event
Items.Content: FR-08: Create Event Description The system must allow creation of event records. Inputs • Event Name • Start Date • End Date Validation Rules • End Date must be greater than or equal to Start Date

Items.Title: Create Job Card
Items.Content: FR-09: Create Job Card Description The system must allow creation of a job card. Inputs • Customer ID • Event ID (optional) Processing • Validate customer exists • Create job record Outputs • Job ID generated

Items.Title: Create Batch
Items.Content: FR-16: Create Batch Description The system must group service items into a batch. Validation Rules • Items must not be duplicated

Items.Title: Create Payment Voucher
Items.Content: FR-17: Create Payment Voucher Description The system must create payment vouchers for vendors. Inputs • Batch ID Processing • Calculate payable amount

Items.Title: Create Invoice
Items.Content: FR-18: Create Invoice Description The system must generate invoices. Validation Rules • Must link to valid job

Items.Title: Create Receipt
Items.Content: FR-20: Create Receipt Description The system must record payments. Validation Rules • Amount must not exceed invoice

Items.Title: Update Customer
Items.Content:
FR-04: Update Customer Description The system must allow updating existing customer details. 

Inputs • Customer ID • Updated fields Processing • Verify customer exists • Update record Outputs • Success: Updated customer • Failure: Not found / validation error

Items.Title: Update Job Card
Items.Content: FR-10: Update Job Card Description The system must allow updating job card details. Validation Rules • Job must not be closed

Items.Title: Update Service Item
Items.Content: FR-13: Update Service Item Description The system must allow updating service details.

Items.Title: Retrieve Customer
Items.Content: FR-05: Retrieve Customer Description The system must allow retrieval of customer details. Inputs • Customer ID Processing • Fetch customer record Outputs • Success: Customer details • Failure: Not found

Items.Title: Retrieve Vendor
Items.Content: FR-07: Retrieve Vendor Description The system must allow retrieval of vendor details.

Items.Title: Close Job Card
Items.Content:
FR-11: Close Job Card Description The system must allow closing of job cards. Processing • Update status to Closed Validation Rules 

• Closed jobs cannot be modified

Items.Title: Add Service Item
Items.Content: FR-12: Add Service Item Description The system must allow adding service items to a job. Inputs • Job ID • Service Type • Cost Processing • Validate job exists • Add service record Validation Rules • Cost must be ≥ 0

Items.Title: Service Type Validity A service item must be classified under a valid supported service category. Supported Types • Transportation • Hotel • Visa • Miscellaneous
Items.Content: BRULE-21: Service Type Validity A service item must be classified under a valid supported service category. Supported Types • Transportation • Hotel • Visa • Miscellaneous

Items.Title: Service Editability A service item may only be edited if its parent job card remains eligible for modification and the service has not been locked by downstream financial
Items.Content: BRULE-22: Service Editability A service item may only be edited if its parent job card remains eligible for modification and the service has not been locked by downstream financial processing.

Items.Title: Service Deletion Restriction A service item must not be removed if it has already been included in a finalized financial process unless the system supports controlled reversal procedures.
Items.Content: BRULE-23: Service Deletion Restriction A service item must not be removed if it has already been included in a finalized financial process unless the system supports controlled reversal procedures.

Items.Title: Visa Service Validity Visa-related services must include the required identifying or
Items.Content:
BRULE-27: Visa Service Validity Visa-related services must include the required identifying or processing information defined by the service model. 

Explanation: Mandatory visa-specific fields must be validated before save.

Items.Title: Remove Service Item
Items.Content: FR-14: Remove Service Item Description The system must allow removal of service items. Validation Rules • Cannot remove if already invoiced

Items.Title: Calculate Job Cost
Items.Content: FR-15: Calculate Job Cost Description The system must calculate total cost of a job. Processing • Sum all service costs Output • Total cost

Items.Title: Generate Reports
Items.Content:
FR-21: Generate Reports Description The system must generate reports. Types • Operational • Financial • P&L 

4. Business Rules and Validation Requirements

• Reporting and audit rules Each rule is assigned an identifier for traceability.

Items.Title: Mandatory Data Integrity All records created, updated, or processed by the system must preserve referential and transactional integrity across related business entities.
Items.Content: BRULE-01: Mandatory Data Integrity All records created, updated, or processed by the system must preserve referential and transactional integrity across related business entities. Explanation: The system must not allow orphaned records, broken references, or inconsistent cross-module updates.

Items.Title: Mandatory Cost Value Every service item that contributes to business cost must contain a valid cost value.
Items.Content: BRULE-24: Mandatory Cost Value Every service item that contributes to business cost must contain a valid cost value. Validation Requirements • Cost must be numeric. • Cost must not be negative.

Items.Title: Valid Entity Reference A transaction must not reference a customer, vendor, event, job card, batch, invoice, or other entity unless that entity already exists and is valid for use. Explanation: The system must verify the existence of referenced entities before
Items.Content: BRULE-02: Valid Entity Reference A transaction must not reference a customer, vendor, event, job card, batch, invoice, or other entity unless that entity already exists and is valid for use. Explanation: The system must verify the existence of referenced entities before processing the request.

Items.Title: Valid Batch Requirement for Voucher Creation A payment voucher must be linked to valid batch data or another valid source of payable operational items.
Items.Content: BRULE-39: Valid Batch Requirement for Voucher Creation A payment voucher must be linked to valid batch data or another valid source of payable operational items.

Items.Title: Valid Invoice Requirement for Receipt A receipt must be linked to a valid payable invoice or supported financial source.
Items.Content: BRULE-51: Valid Invoice Requirement for Receipt A receipt must be linked to a valid payable invoice or supported financial source.

Items.Title: Controlled Modification Records that have reached a finalized, closed, processed, or voided state must be subject to controlled modification rules.
Items.Content: BRULE-03: Controlled Modification Records that have reached a finalized, closed, processed, or voided state must be subject to controlled modification rules. Explanation: Not every record can remain editable forever. Some records must become restricted after reaching a defined state.

Items.Title: Audit Preservation Where financial or operational actions have audit significance, the system must preserve transaction history and avoid destructive
Items.Content: BRULE-04: Audit Preservation Where financial or operational actions have audit significance, the system must preserve transaction history and avoid destructive processing that removes historical traceability. Explanation: Important actions such as voiding financial records should preserve evidence of prior existence and status.

Items.Title: Authenticated Access Required All protected API operations must require successful authentication.
Items.Content: BRULE-05: Authenticated Access Required All protected API operations must require successful authentication. Validation Requirement • Requests without valid credentials or token must be rejected.

Items.Title: Identity Context Every authorized transaction must be processed within the context of the authenticated user identity.
Items.Content: BRULE-07: Identity Context Every authorized transaction must be processed within the context of the authenticated user identity. Explanation: Where required, the system must associate changes or actions with the authenticated user.

Items.Title: Job Card Identity Each job card must have a unique identifier.
Items.Content: BRULE-14: Job Card Identity Each job card must have a unique identifier. Explanation: The system must ensure that each job card is uniquely traceable.

Items.Title: Protected Deletion of Master Data A customer, vendor, or event must not be deleted if it is actively referenced by downstream business transactions unless the system explicitly supports safe deactivation instead of deletion.
Items.Content: BRULE-11: Protected Deletion of Master Data A customer, vendor, or event must not be deleted if it is actively referenced by downstream business transactions unless the system explicitly supports safe deactivation instead of deletion. Explanation: This prevents broken references and historical inconsistencies.

Items.Title: Active Usage Constraint Only active or valid master data records should be available for new downstream transactions.
Items.Content: BRULE-12: Active Usage Constraint Only active or valid master data records should be available for new downstream transactions. Explanation: Inactive entities may remain visible historically but should not necessarily be eligible for new business use.

Items.Title: Job Card Lifecycle Control A job card must operate within defined lifecycle states. Minimum Expected States • Open • Closed
Items.Content: BRULE-15: Job Card Lifecycle Control A job card must operate within defined lifecycle states. Minimum Expected States • Open • Closed

Items.Title: Closed Job Restriction A closed job card must not accept new operational service items or unrestricted modifications.
Items.Content: BRULE-17: Closed Job Restriction A closed job card must not accept new operational service items or unrestricted modifications. Explanation: Closing a job card indicates operational completion or administrative lock.

Items.Title: Job Closure Preconditions The system may require certain business conditions to be satisfied before a job card can be closed. Possible Conditions • Required operational data completed • Financial dependencies reviewed • No invalid incomplete service lines
Items.Content:
BRULE-18: Job Closure Preconditions The system may require certain business conditions to be satisfied before a job card can be closed. Possible Conditions • Required operational data completed • Financial dependencies reviewed • No invalid incomplete service lines 

Where such preconditions are enforced by the implementation, the API must validate them prior to closure.

Items.Title: Deletion Restriction for Financially Used Jobs A job card must not be deleted if it has already contributed to invoices, batches, vouchers, receipts, or other financially significant downstream records.
Items.Content: BRULE-19: Deletion Restriction for Financially Used Jobs A job card must not be deleted if it has already contributed to invoices, batches, vouchers, receipts, or other financially significant downstream records. Explanation: Historical financial traceability must be preserved.

Items.Title: Service-to-Job Relationship Every operational service item must belong to a valid job card.
Items.Content: BRULE-20: Service-to-Job Relationship Every operational service item must belong to a valid job card. Validation Requirements • Service creation must fail if JobId is invalid or missing.

Items.Title: Transportation Route Validity For transportation entries, pickup and drop-off information must be valid and meaningful.
Items.Content: BRULE-25: Transportation Route Validity For transportation entries, pickup and drop-off information must be valid and meaningful. Validation Requirements • Pickup and drop-off must not both be blank. • Where both are provided, they should not represent the same unusable route unless explicitly allowed.

Items.Title: Hotel Stay Validity For hotel entries, stay duration must be logically valid.
Items.Content: BRULE-26: Hotel Stay Validity For hotel entries, stay duration must be logically valid. Validation Requirements • Check-out date must be later than or equal to check-in date, depending on the defined booking model. • Room-related quantities must be positive if quantity fields are used.

Items.Title: Miscellaneous Service Clarity Miscellaneous items must contain sufficient descriptive information to justify their existence and financial value. Validation Requirements •
Items.Content: BRULE-28: Miscellaneous Service Clarity Miscellaneous items must contain sufficient descriptive information to justify their existence and financial value. Validation Requirements • Description must not be empty. • Cost must remain valid.

Items.Title: Non-Negative Cost Rule All cost-bearing service entries must have non-negative amounts.
Items.Content: BRULE-29: Non-Negative Cost Rule All cost-bearing service entries must have non-negative amounts.

Items.Title: Recalculation on Change The total cost of a job card must be recalculated whenever a cost-bearing service item is added, updated, or removed.
Items.Content: BRULE-30: Recalculation on Change The total cost of a job card must be recalculated whenever a cost-bearing service item is added, updated, or removed.

Items.Title: Aggregated Total Accuracy The total job cost must equal the sum of all valid included service item costs associated with that job.
Items.Content: BRULE-31: Aggregated Total Accuracy The total job cost must equal the sum of all valid included service item costs associated with that job.

Items.Title: Financial Consistency Operational totals used in downstream financial
Items.Content: BRULE-32: Financial Consistency Operational totals used in downstream financial processing must remain consistent with the relevant included service records. Explanation: The system must avoid mismatches between operational values and financial values derived from them.

Items.Title: Invalid Numeric Rejection The system must reject invalid numeric values such as non-numeric costs, malformed decimals, or values outside supported limits.
Items.Content: BRULE-33: Invalid Numeric Rejection The system must reject invalid numeric values such as non-numeric costs, malformed decimals, or values outside supported limits.

Items.Title: Eligibility for Batching Only eligible operational items may be added to a batch. Typical Eligibility Conditions • Item exists • Item is valid • Item is not already processed in another conflicting financial workflow
Items.Content: BRULE-34: Eligibility for Batching Only eligible operational items may be added to a batch. Typical Eligibility Conditions • Item exists • Item is valid • Item is not already processed in another conflicting financial workflow

Items.Title: Single Batch Membership An item must not belong to multiple active or conflicting batches when the business process expects exclusive grouping.
Items.Content: BRULE-35: Single Batch Membership An item must not belong to multiple active or conflicting batches when the business process expects exclusive grouping.

Items.Title: Empty Batch Prevention The system must not create or persist a financial batch without at least one valid item.
Items.Content: BRULE-36: Empty Batch Prevention The system must not create or persist a financial batch without at least one valid item.

Items.Title: Processed Batch Restriction Once a batch has been processed into downstream financial transactions, unrestricted editing or item replacement should not be allowed.
Items.Content: BRULE-38: Processed Batch Restriction Once a batch has been processed into downstream financial transactions, unrestricted editing or item replacement should not be allowed.

Items.Title: Voucher Amount Accuracy The value of a payment voucher must match the payable amount derived from the included operational items according to business rules.
Items.Content: BRULE-40: Voucher Amount Accuracy The value of a payment voucher must match the payable amount derived from the included operational items according to business rules.

Items.Title: Voucher Traceability A voucher must remain traceable to the items, batches, or services that generated the payable amount.
Items.Content: BRULE-43: Voucher Traceability A voucher must remain traceable to the items, batches, or services that generated the payable amount.

Items.Title: Duplicate Voucher Prevention The system must prevent duplicate or conflicting payment voucher creation for the same payable business content unless intentional partial or revised voucher behavior is explicitly supported.
Items.Content: BRULE-41: Duplicate Voucher Prevention The system must prevent duplicate or conflicting payment voucher creation for the same payable business content unless intentional partial or revised voucher behavior is explicitly supported.

Items.Title: Duplicate Billing Prevention The system must prevent double invoicing of the same billable content unless explicitly allowed by the business model.
Items.Content: BRULE-50: Duplicate Billing Prevention The system must prevent double invoicing of the same billable content unless explicitly allowed by the business model.

Items.Title: Finalized Invoice Restriction Once finalized, an invoice must not be freely modified.
Items.Content:
BRULE-46: Finalized Invoice Restriction Once finalized, an invoice must not be freely modified. 

Explanation: Subsequent correction should occur through controlled actions such as voiding or issuance of replacement records if supported.

Items.Title: Voided Invoice Preservation Voiding an invoice must preserve the invoice record and its historical relationship rather than erase it.
Items.Content: BRULE-47: Voided Invoice Preservation Voiding an invoice must preserve the invoice record and its historical relationship rather than erase it.

Items.Title: Voided or Invalid Target Restriction The system must prevent receipt application to a voided, invalid, or otherwise non-receivable financial document unless business rules explicitly permit it.
Items.Content: BRULE-53: Voided or Invalid Target Restriction The system must prevent receipt application to a voided, invalid, or otherwise non-receivable financial document unless business rules explicitly permit it.

Items.Title: Profit and Loss Consistency Profit and loss reporting must be based on accurate revenue and cost figures derived from valid business records.
Items.Content: BRULE-57: Profit and Loss Consistency Profit and loss reporting must be based on accurate revenue and cost figures derived from valid business records.

Items.Title: Historical Integrity Historical reports must preserve prior business events and should not be distorted by destructive deletion of financially relevant records.
Items.Content: BRULE-58: Historical Integrity Historical reports must preserve prior business events and should not be distorted by destructive deletion of financially relevant records.

Items.Title: Uninvoiced Identification Uninvoiced reporting must correctly identify valid business content that has not yet been invoiced according to the billing rules.
Items.Content: BRULE-59: Uninvoiced Identification Uninvoiced reporting must correctly identify valid business content that has not yet been invoiced according to the billing rules.

Items.Title: Auditability of Financial Events Financially significant actions such as invoice creation, voiding, receipt recording, and voucher creation must remain auditable.
Items.Content: BRULE-60: Auditability of Financial Events Financially significant actions such as invoice creation, voiding, receipt recording, and voucher creation must remain auditable.

Items.Title: Clear Validation Feedback When a request fails validation, the system must provide a clear and meaningful error response identifying the reason for rejection.
Items.Content: BRULE-61: Clear Validation Feedback When a request fails validation, the system must provide a clear and meaningful error response identifying the reason for rejection.

Items.Title: Consistent Error Handling Validation failures, missing references, state violations, and unauthorized access must be handled consistently across modules.
Items.Content: BRULE-62: Consistent Error Handling Validation failures, missing references, state violations, and unauthorized access must be handled consistently across modules.

Items.Title: Downstream Dependency Protection A record must not be changed in a way that invalidates already completed downstream
Items.Content: BRULE-63: Downstream Dependency Protection A record must not be changed in a way that invalidates already completed downstream processing unless reversal logic exists.

Items.Title: Operational-to-Financial Continuity Operational records and financial records must remain connected throughout the lifecycle of a business transaction.
Items.Content: BRULE-64: Operational-to-Financial Continuity Operational records and financial records must remain connected throughout the lifecycle of a business transaction.
